title: euphrasia nemorosa var. coerulea
intro: Euphrasia nemorosa var. coerulea, also known as blue marsh gentian, is a variety of the gentian plant species. It is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions, prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive problems, offering health benefits such as improved immune function and reduced inflammation. Euphrasia nemorosa var. coerulea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
